checks.name: SSH hardening
checks.details: PermitRootLogin enabled: 'PermitRootLogin yes'. → Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config → PermitRootLogin no → sudo systemctl restart ssh

checks.name: Firewall enabled
checks.details: ufw inactive. → sudo ufw enable && sudo ufw allow 22/tcp && sudo ufw status

checks.name: Minimal services running
checks.details: 34 services running. Review with: systemctl list-units

attack_paths.name: Server → Internal Services
attack_paths.description: Multiple server misconfigs increase attack surface

patches.instructions: Open the printed config snippet and add the appropriate block to your Flask app config or Django settings.py. These settings require HTTPS to be active — enabling Secure cookies on an HTTP-only site will break sessions.
For Flask: install flask-wtf and initialise CSRFProtect. For Django: ensure CsrfViewMiddleware is in MIDDLEWARE and {% csrf_token %} is in all POST forms.

1. Save this script as APP-ADMIN-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x APP-ADMIN-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: ./APP-ADMIN-001.sh
4. Review the planned changes
5. Apply the fix: ./APP-ADMIN-001.sh --apply
6. Verify admin endpoints return 403 status
patches.verification: curl -I http://localhost/admin && curl -I http://localhost/debug && curl -I http://localhost/test && curl -I http://localhost/wp-admin

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-rate-limiting.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-rate-limiting.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./fix-rate-limiting.sh --dry-run
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./fix-rate-limiting.sh --apply
6. Monitor /var/log/nginx/rate_limit.log for rate limiting events

1. Save the patch file as APP-PASS-001.py
2. Make it executable: chmod +x APP-PASS-001.py
3. Run dry-run first: sudo python3 APP-PASS-001.py
4. Review the changes that will be made
5. Apply the fix: sudo python3 APP-PASS-001.py --apply
6. Test password policy with a new user or passwd command
7. Verify backups were created in case rollback is needed
patches.verification: sudo python3 -c "import subprocess; result = subprocess.run(['pwscore'], input='TestPass123!', text=True, capture_output=True); print('Password policy active:' if result.returncode == 0 else 'Policy may need adjustment')"

patches.instructions: 1. Open your nginx server configuration (usually /etc/nginx/sites-available/your-site). 2. Add the add_header line inside your HTTPS server block. 3. Run `sudo nginx -t` to verify. 4. Run `sudo systemctl reload nginx` to apply.

1. Save this script as WS-SEC-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x WS-SEC-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first: sudo ./WS-SEC-001.sh --dry-run
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: sudo ./WS-SEC-001.sh --apply
6. The script will create backups, add security headers, and reload nginx safely
Add these directives to your nginx HTTPS server block. After applying, verify with SSL Labs: https://www.ssllabs.com/ssltest/analyze.html?d=your-domain.com

1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-ws-srv-001.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-ws-srv-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./fix-ws-srv-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: ./fix-ws-srv-001.sh --apply
6. The script will backup your config, modify it, test it, and reload Nginx
patches.verification: curl -I http://localhost 2>/dev/null | grep -i server || echo 'Server header hidden successfully'

patches.instructions:
1. Save this script to a file (e.g., fix-ws-limit-001.sh)
2. Make it executable: chmod +x fix-ws-limit-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: ./fix-ws-limit-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the fix: ./fix-ws-limit-001.sh --apply
6. The script will automatically backup configs, test nginx config, and reload if successful

1. Save this script as HOST-SSH-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-SSH-001.sh
3. Run dry-run first to see changes: sudo ./HOST-SSH-001.sh --dry-run
4. Apply the fix: sudo ./HOST-SSH-001.sh --apply
5. The script will create a backup, update the SSH config, validate it, and restart the SSH service

1. Save this script as HOST-FW-001.sh
2. Make it executable: chmod +x HOST-FW-001.sh
3. Run in dry-run mode first: sudo ./HOST-FW-001.sh
4. Review the proposed changes
5. Apply the changes: sudo ./HOST-FW-001.sh --apply
6. If you use a different SSH port, specify it: sudo ./HOST-FW-001.sh --apply --ssh-port 2222
Review running services with systemctl and disable those not required for the web stack.
